profile.name: Dan Sherman
roles: Alleged Intuitive Communicator (IC), Project Preserve Destiny
Author - Above Black: Project Preserve Destiny (1998)
profile.summary: 12-year USAF veteran who served as an Electronic Intelligence (ELINT) specialist with NSA affiliation and Top Secret/SCI clearances. In his 1998 self-published book 'Above Black,' Sherman claims that in 1992 at NSA's Fort Meade facility he was separately briefed that his ELINT role was cover for his real assignment as an 'Intuitive Communicator' - a government program allegedly training genetically selected personnel to relay communications from extraterrestrial entities. He claims approximately 10 months of contact with an ET he nicknamed 'Bones,' transmitting what he interpreted as abduction-related data. No documentary evidence for Project Preserve Destiny exists in the public record. His verifiable military background is considered credible; the IC claims are not independently corroborated.
education: NSA Intermediate ELINT Course, Fort Meade, Maryland (1992)
early_career: Enlisted in the U.S. Air Force at age 18 in 1982 as Security Police
Stationed at Osan Air Base, South Korea; learned about ELINT career path from fellow airman
Completed ELINT tech school c. 1990; transitioned from Security Police to Electronic Intelligence specialty
Assigned to Offutt AFB, Nebraska (SAC Headquarters); San Vito dei Normanni, Italy; Buckley ANGB, Colorado; temporary duty in Holland and Germany

key_events.event: Enlisted in the U.S. Air Force at age 18, directly out of high school. Initial specialty: Security Police. Earned Top Secret and SCI clearances over the course of his service.

Stationed at Osan Air Base, South Korea. Met a fellow airman who recommended Electronic Intelligence (ELINT) as a career path. Began the transition process that would eventually lead to NSA affiliation.

Completed ELINT technical school. Began assignments as an ELINT specialist, analyzing electromagnetic energy from C-vans at operational bases in support of NSA intelligence operations. Postings included Offutt AFB (Nebraska), San Vito dei Normanni (Italy), Buckley ANGB (Colorado), and temporary duty in Holland and Germany.

Sent to Fort Meade, Maryland for an NSA intermediate-level ELINT course. Claims that during this assignment, a captain separately briefed him that his ELINT role was cover for a classified program called 'Project Preserve Destiny' - and that he had been selected as an 'Intuitive Communicator' (IC) based on alleged genetic modifications made to him in utero. Claims IC training began at a specialized computer terminal at the NSA facility.

Began alleged operational period as Intuitive Communicator. Claims contact with two extraterrestrial entities he nicknamed 'Spock' (brief contact) and 'Bones' (sustained contact over approximately 10 months, 75+ messages received). States the content of messages consisted largely of data he interpreted as correlated to human abduction events - latitudes and longitudes, residual pain metrics. Was instructed not to question the content he was transmitting.

Forced early discharge after approximately 12 years of service. States he sought discharge deliberately after becoming psychologically disturbed by the content of the ET communications and after handlers refused to relieve him of the IC assignment. Awarded Air Force Commendation Medal and Air Force Achievement Medal.

Published 'Above Black: Project Preserve Destiny' online. The book provides a detailed account of his USAF career through the alleged Project Preserve Destiny assignment and subsequent discharge. Print edition followed in January 1998 through One Team Publishing (Wilsonville, Oregon).

Print edition of 'Above Black' published. Conducted early interviews on Coast to Coast AM (Art Bell) and Sightings on the Air (Jeff Rense). The book circulated within UFO and exopolitics communities and was eventually shipped to over 45 countries.

Interviewed by Bill Ryan and Kerry Cassidy of Project Camelot in Portland, Oregon. The 'Way Above Black' interview became his most widely cited appearance, released as MP3, video, and full transcript. Remains the primary extended record of his claims.

Researcher Noah Hradek filed FOIA requests with the Air Force regarding 'Project Preserve Destiny.' The Air Force declined to process the request, citing NSA jurisdiction, and referred the matter to the NSA. The NSA invoked Executive Order 13526, issuing a standard Glomar-style response that neither confirmed nor denied any such program's existence.

Appeared on Jesse Michels' American Alchemy podcast - a high-profile interview that significantly renewed mainstream UAP community interest in his account. The episode received wide circulation on social media and introduced his claims to a new generation of UAP researchers.

credibility.note: Sherman has a verifiable 12-year USAF career with appropriate security clearances and real NSA-affiliated ELINT assignments - the factual foundation of his account is solid. His presentation is notably restrained: he limits himself strictly to firsthand claims, acknowledges the possibility his experience was a deception operation, and has not built a commercial enterprise around his story. However, there is zero documentary evidence for Project Preserve Destiny as he describes it, no independent witnesses to the IC training or ET communications, and the core mechanism - genetic modification in utero enabling telepathic ET communication - is extraordinary, unfalsifiable, and structurally similar to channeling community narratives circulating in the same era. He is not cited by serious investigative journalists, congressional researchers, or mainstream UAP investigators.
supporting: 12-year USAF career with verified Top Secret/SCI clearances and real NSA-affiliated ELINT postings - the verifiable portions of his background are consistent and credible
Posting locations (Osan, Offutt, San Vito, Buckley, Fort Meade) are all real and consistent with ELINT operations of the era
Presentation style is unusually restrained - he limits claims to direct personal experience, declines to speculate beyond what he witnessed, and openly acknowledges he may have been deceived
Has not built a significant commercial enterprise around his story; no lecture circuit, no ongoing media career, a single self-published book
The structural description of layered classification and programs hidden inside cover programs is accurate as a general description of how intelligence compartmentalization works
FOIA referral of his claims to the NSA (rather than a simple denial) is consistent with NSA jurisdiction over classified communications programs, though it does not confirm PPD
USAF Air Force Commendation Medal and Achievement Medal - consistent with a genuine service record
Project Preserve Destiny concept was cited in multiple Chinese UFO research contexts, suggesting at least informal international circulation of the claim
contradicting: No documentary evidence for Project Preserve Destiny as an extraterrestrial communications program exists in any declassified or leaked material
No corroborating witnesses to the IC briefing, training, or ET communications have ever come forward
The core mechanism - in utero genetic modification by extraterrestrials enabling telepathic communication - is an extraordinary unfalsifiable claim with no scientific basis
Michael Salla (a sympathetic analyst) himself acknowledged: 'he can't prove he was trained as an intuitive communicator - there's no paper trail'

The claim mirrors the Greys/abduction narrative, STARGATE/remote viewing programs, and contactee stories that were publicly emerging in the same 1990s period - raising the question of narrative contamination
NSA FOIA response was a standard Glomar refusal - neither confirms nor denies the program; it is not evidence of PPD's existence
Primary promoters of his claims include Dr. Richard Boylan, a California psychologist who was stripped of his license for boundary violations - not a credibility-enhancing association
The 2025 Jesse Michels interview describes his rank as 'Technical Sergeant,' while all prior sources including his own account use 'Staff Sergeant' - a minor but unresolved inconsistency

associated_people.name: Gary McKinnon
associated_people.role: British hacker who accessed NASA and DoD systems
associated_people.relationship: Both claimed evidence of classified programs involving extraterrestrial contact within NSA/DoD infrastructure. Sherman's alleged IC program and McKinnon's claimed discovery of non-terrestrial officer rosters are sometimes cited together as parallel insider indicators of the same hidden infrastructure.

associated_people.name: David Grusch
associated_people.relationship: Grusch's 2023 congressional testimony about non-human intelligence programs and crash retrieval operations represents the contemporary institutional parallel to Sherman's 1990s-era individual account. Both describe tiered classified programs inaccessible through standard oversight channels.

disclosures.title: Above Black: Project Preserve Destiny (online release)
disclosures.notes: Initial online publication of his account. Print edition followed January 1998 via One Team Publishing, Wilsonville, Oregon.

disclosures.title: Coast to Coast AM Interview
disclosures.outlet: Coast to Coast AM with Art Bell
disclosures.notes: Early high-profile radio interview following publication of Above Black. Reached the show's large late-night conspiracy/paranormal audience.

disclosures.title: Sightings on the Air Interview
disclosures.outlet: Sightings on the Air with Jeff Rense
disclosures.notes: Interview on UFO-focused radio program during initial book promotion period.

disclosures.title: Way Above Black
disclosures.notes: Extended video and audio interview in Portland, Oregon. The most comprehensive and widely cited account of his claims. Full transcript published by Project Camelot. Re-released on Rumble and Odysee in March 2026.

disclosures.title: Dan Sherman - Project Preserve Destiny
disclosures.notes: Major renewed-visibility interview that introduced Sherman's account to a new generation of UAP researchers. Wide circulation on social media. Available on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, and Audible.

sources.title: Above Black: Project Preserve Destiny (Sherman, 1998)
sources.notes: Primary source - Sherman's own account. Self-published by One Team Publishing, Wilsonville, Oregon.

sources.title: Project Camelot - Dan Sherman 'Way Above Black' Interview Transcript (2007)
sources.notes: Full transcript of the June 2007 Portland interview. Primary source for biographical details and claims.

sources.title: Noah Hradek - FOIA to USAF on Project Preserve Destiny (Medium, 2023)
sources.notes: Documents the FOIA request and Air Force referral to NSA. Primary source for the government FOIA response.

sources.title: Dan Sherman Official Website - aboveblack.com
sources.notes: Sherman's own site; includes book information, interview history, and background material.

sources.title: We Are The Mighty - This Airman Claims His Top-Secret Duty Was to Talk to Aliens
sources.notes: Mainstream military journalism covering Sherman's background and claims.

career_connections.relationship: Stone is the closest structural parallel - a U.S. Army NCO who claimed direct operational involvement in classified crash retrieval programs during the same Cold War era. Both describe above-black programs hidden within legitimate military roles, and both emerged as public witnesses through the late-1990s UFO disclosure community.

McKinnon independently claimed to find evidence of NSA/DoD ET communications infrastructure by accessing military networks from the outside. Sherman claims to have been operating inside that same infrastructure during the same era. Placing this connection at the IC communications phase makes the conceptual parallel explicit.

Greer was building the Disclosure Project simultaneously with Sherman's 1997 publication. Greer's network actively circulated Above Black as part of the broader witness testimony corpus. This branching point represents Sherman entering the organized disclosure community and his account becoming part of Greer's collected insider testimony.

Hare was also a Project Camelot interview subject during the same period. Both are insider witnesses whose accounts of classified government programs - Hare at NASA, Sherman at NSA - circulated primarily through alternative media channels including Project Camelot rather than mainstream journalism.

Grusch's 2023 congressional testimony about non-human intelligence programs and tiered above-black access represents the institutional evolution of precisely the kind of claim Sherman made in 1998. Sherman was among the first to publicly describe these programs; Grusch brought similar claims before a formal congressional hearing a quarter century later.
